summary: The PoC demonstrates SSRF vulnerability in the workflow import feature, confirming that internal services and mock cloud metadata endpoints can be accessed through the from-url API.
expected_security_impact: The PoC should attempt to import workflows from mock cloud metadata endpoints (port 9999) and internal API endpoints (port 8080), demonstrating unauthorized access to internal services and potential credential theft.
actual_observed_result: The PoC successfully accessed fake cloud metadata endpoint and internal admin API through the workflow import URL parameter, demonstrating SSRF capability to reach internal network services that should not be accessible from external APIs.
verification_method: Verification checks for proof files indicating successful SSRF access to the metadata endpoint and internal API, confirming that responses from internal services were retrieved through the vulnerable endpoint.
evidence_produced_by_run: Mock cloud metadata endpoint on port 9999 was successfully accessed via SSRF
Internal admin API on port 8080 was successfully accessed via SSRF
Fake AWS metadata (account ID, secret keys) was retrieved through the vulnerability
Internal service credentials and configuration data were exposed via SSRF
The from-url endpoint accepts arbitrary URLs without validation or internal network restrictions
limitations_or_blockers: Full exploitation requires actual cloud environment or internal network topology
The PoC uses mock services to demonstrate the vulnerability mechanism
Real-world impact depends on what internal services are available in the target deployment

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the n8n repository from GitHub and mounts it read-only at /target-repo
reproduction.pinned_commit_or_ref: HEAD (latest commit at time of execution)
